 {"id":"caam-3ef","title":"Health status calculation logic","description":"# Health Status Calculation

## Purpose
Calculate profile health status (🟢🟡🔴) based on token expiry, errors, and penalties.

## Background (from codex-pool)
```go
score = headroom * planBonus * creditBonus - penalty
```

For caam v1, we simplify to status tiers with an internal numeric score.

## Technical Requirements

### Status Enum
```go
type HealthStatus int

const (
    StatusUnknown  HealthStatus = iota // ⚪ Cannot determine
    StatusHealthy                      // 🟢 Good to use
    StatusWarning                      // 🟡 Expiring soon or errors
    StatusCritical                     // 🔴 Expired or many errors
)

func (s HealthStatus) Icon() string
func (s HealthStatus) String() string
```

### Scoring Algorithm
```go
func CalculateHealth(h *ProfileHealth) (HealthStatus, float64) {
    score := 0.0
    now := time.Now()
    
    // Factor 1: Token expiry (primary)
    if h.TokenExpiresAt.IsZero() {
        // Unknown expiry - neutral
    } else if h.TokenExpiresAt.Before(now) {
        score -= 1.0  // Expired
    } else {
        ttl := h.TokenExpiresAt.Sub(now)
        switch {
        case ttl > time.Hour:
            score += 1.0
        case ttl > 15*time.Minute:
            score += 0.5
        default:
            // < 15 min: no bonus
        }
    }
    
    // Factor 2: Recent errors
    switch {
    case h.ErrorCount1h == 0:
        score += 0.3
    case h.ErrorCount1h <= 2:
        // Neutral
    default:
        score -= 0.5
    }
    
    // Factor 3: Plan type bonus
    switch h.PlanType {
    case "enterprise":
        score += 0.3
    case "pro", "team":
        score += 0.2
    }
    
    // Factor 4: Penalty (from errors, with decay)
    score -= h.Penalty
    
    // Convert to status
    status := StatusHealthy
    if score < 0 {
        status = StatusCritical
    } else if score < 0.5 {
        status = StatusWarning
    }
    
    return status, score
}
```

### Thresholds (Configurable)
```go
type HealthConfig struct {
    TokenExpiryWarningMinutes int     // Default: 60
    TokenExpiryCriticalMinutes int    // Default: 15
    ErrorCountWarning         int     // Default: 2
    ErrorCountCritical        int     // Default: 5
}
```

## User-Facing Display
```
🟢 Healthy   - Good to use
🟡 Warning   - Token expiring soon or recent errors
🔴 Critical  - Token expired or many errors
⚪ Unknown   - Cannot determine status
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HealthStatus enum with Icon() and String()
- [ ] CalculateHealth function with documented algorithm
- [ ] Configurable thresholds via HealthConfig
- [ ] Unit tests covering all status transitions
- [ ] Edge cases: zero time, negative penalty, nil health

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/health/status.go` (new)
- `internal/health/status_test.go` (new)

## Dependencies

 {"id":"caam-3nx","title":"EPIC: Data Safety & Recovery","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:42:40.84068493-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:54:44.596221955-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:54:44.596221955-05:00","close_reason":"Scope delivered: original backup, protected system profiles, smart auto-backup, uninstall restore+cleanup.","comments":[{"id":36,"issue_id":"caam-3nx","author":"ubuntu","text":"Purpose: Ensure users can never lose their original authentication state and can fully reverse any changes made by caam. This is critical for user trust and adoption.

Background: Currently, caam has a dangerous gap: if a user runs caam activate foo without first backing up their current auth, the original files are overwritten and permanently lost. This violates the principle of least surprise and could cause real harm.

Scope: (1) Auto-backup of original state on first use, (2) Protected profile naming conventions, (3) Smart auto-backup before any switch, (4) Uninstall command.

Success Criteria: User can NEVER lose original auth through normal usage; Clear distinction between user/system profiles; Single command to reverse all changes; Zero additional friction.
","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:43:17Z"},{"id":58,"issue_id":"caam-3nx","author":"ubuntu","text":"Completed Data Safety & Recovery scope: (1) first-activate safety net backs up pre-caam auth into reserved _original per tool; (2) protected system profile naming (leading underscore) prevents user overwrite; (3) smart auto-backup-before-switch for unsaved state + rotation; (4) uninstall command restores from _original and cleans up. Added/updated tests across activate, authfile rotation, and uninstall.","created_at":"2025-12-18T04:54:32Z"}]}


This is foundational - other safety features depend on these config options.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:43:31.755611107-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:26:05.213751017-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:26:05.213751017-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented SafetyConfig with auto_backup_before_switch (always|smart|never) and max_auto_backups. Added validation and tests.","comments":[{"id":39,"issue_id":"caam-3w5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - simplified config schema:

```yaml
safety:
  auto_backup_before_switch: smart  # "always" | "smart" | "never"
  max_auto_backups: 5               # Keep last N auto-backups, rotate older
```

## Changes from Original Design

REMOVED - `auto_backup_original`: This is now ALWAYS enabled, not configurable.
Rationale: Making it optional defeats the entire safety purpose. The one-time backup
of original state has zero downside and prevents catastrophic data loss.

REMOVED - `protected_profile_prefix`: Now hardcoded as `_`.
Rationale: Making this configurable adds complexity with no benefit. The `_` prefix
is a standard convention (like hidden files) and changing it could cause confusion.

## What Remains
- `auto_backup_before_switch`: Controls smart backup on every switch (optional)
- `max_auto_backups`: Limits accumulation of timestamped backups

## Files
- internal/config/spm_config.go: Add SafetyConfig struct (simplified)
- internal/config/spm_config_test.go: Test defaults and validation

-{"id":"caam-3wx","title":"Create sync infrastructure and machine discovery","description":"# Task: Create sync infrastructure and machine discovery

## Context
Build the foundational infrastructure for multi-machine sync: the sync package, machine definitions, and discovery mechanisms.

## Package Structure

### Location
`internal/sync/` (new package)

### Files
```
internal/sync/
├── machine.go         # Machine type and operations
├── pool.go            # Sync pool management
├── discovery.go       # Machine discovery (SSH config, CSV)
├── csv.go             # CSV file handling
├── state.go           # Sync state persistence
├── identity.go        # Local machine identity
└── sync_test.go       # Tests
```

## Types

### Machine
```go
type Machine struct {
    ID           string    `json:"id"`           // Unique identifier (generated UUID)
    Name         string    `json:"name"`         // Friendly name
    Address      string    `json:"address"`      // IP, hostname, or hostname:port
    Port         int       `json:"port"`         // SSH port (default: 22)
    SSHUser      string    `json:"ssh_user"`     // SSH username (default: current user)
    SSHKeyPath   string    `json:"ssh_key_path"` // Path to SSH private key
    RemotePath   string    `json:"remote_path"`  // Path to caam data on remote
    Status       string    `json:"status"`       // online, offline, syncing, error
    LastSync     time.Time `json:"last_sync"`    // Last successful sync
    LastError    string    `json:"last_error"`   // Last error message
    LastErrorAt  time.Time `json:"last_error_at"`// When last error occurred
    AddedAt      time.Time `json:"added_at"`     // When machine was added
    Source       string    `json:"source"`       // "ssh_config", "csv", "manual"
}

// ParseAddress handles "hostname", "hostname:port", "user@hostname:port"
func ParseAddress(addr string) (host string, port int, user string)
```

### Local Machine Identity
Each machine needs a unique identity for sync protocol:
```go
type LocalIdentity struct {
    ID        string    `json:"id"`         // UUID, generated once
    Hostname  string    `json:"hostname"`   // Machine hostname
    CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"` // When identity was created
}

func GetOrCreateLocalIdentity() (*LocalIdentity, error)
```
Stored in: `~/.local/share/caam/sync/identity.json`

### SyncPool
```go
type SyncPool struct {
    LocalMachineID string              `json:"local_machine_id"`
    Machines       map[string]*Machine `json:"machines"`
    Enabled        bool                `json:"enabled"`
    AutoSync       bool                `json:"auto_sync"`  // Default: false (opt-in)
    LastFullSync   time.Time           `json:"last_full_sync"`
}

func NewSyncPool() *SyncPool
func (p *SyncPool) AddMachine(m *Machine) error
func (p *SyncPool) RemoveMachine(id string) error
func (p *SyncPool) GetMachine(id string) *Machine
func (p *SyncPool) GetMachineByName(name string) *Machine
func (p *SyncPool) ListMachines() []*Machine
func (p *SyncPool) Save() error
func (p *SyncPool) Load() error
```

## Machine Discovery

### SSH Config Parser
Parse `~/.ssh/config` to extract host definitions:

```go
func DiscoverFromSSHConfig() ([]*Machine, error)
```

Parses entries like:
```
Host work-laptop
    HostName 192.168.1.100
    Port 22
    User jeff
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/work_key
```

Produces:
```go
&Machine{
    Name:       "work-laptop",
    Address:    "192.168.1.100",
    Port:       22,
    SSHUser:    "jeff",
    SSHKeyPath: "~/.ssh/work_key",
    Source:     "ssh_config",
}
```

**Filtering**: Skip known non-caam hosts:
- github.com, gitlab.com, bitbucket.org
- Hosts with ProxyJump (complex setups)
- Wildcards (Host *)

### CSV File

#### Location (CHANGED from ~/)
`~/.caam/sync_machines.csv`

This keeps all caam config in ~/.caam/ for consistency.

#### Format
```csv
machine_name,address,ssh_key_path
work-laptop,192.168.1.100,~/.ssh/id_ed25519
home-desktop,10.0.0.50:2222,~/.ssh/home_key
cloud-vm,user@34.123.45.67,~/.ssh/cloud_rsa
```

Notes:
- Address can include port: `hostname:port`
- Address can include user: `user@hostname` or `user@hostname:port`
- SSH key path supports ~ expansion
- SSH user defaults to current user if not in address
- Port defaults to 22 if not specified

#### Auto-creation
On first sync command, if file doesn't exist:
1. Create file with header row and comments
2. Optionally pre-populate from SSH config (filtered)
3. Print message about editing the file

```go
func EnsureCSVFile() (created bool, err error)
func LoadFromCSV() ([]*Machine, error)
func SaveToCSV(machines []*Machine) error
func MergeDiscoveredMachines(existing, discovered []*Machine) []*Machine
```

#### Full CSV with comments
```csv
# CAAM Sync Pool Configuration
# 
# Add machines you want to sync auth tokens with.
# 
# Format: machine_name,address,ssh_key_path
#   - address: IP or hostname, optionally with :port and/or user@
#   - ssh_key_path: path to SSH private key (~ expands to home)
#
# Examples:
#   work-laptop,192.168.1.100,~/.ssh/id_ed25519
#   home-desktop,10.0.0.50:2222,~/.ssh/home_key
#   cloud-vm,jeff@34.123.45.67,~/.ssh/cloud_rsa
#
machine_name,address,ssh_key_path
```

## State Persistence

### Location
`~/.local/share/caam/sync/`

### Files
- `identity.json` - This machine's unique identity
- `pool.json` - Sync pool configuration and machine list
- `queue.json` - Pending sync operations (for retry)
- `history.json` - Recent sync log (last 1000 entries)

```go
type SyncState struct {
    Identity *LocalIdentity
    Pool     *SyncPool
    Queue    *SyncQueue
    History  *SyncHistory
}

func NewSyncState(basePath string) *SyncState
func (s *SyncState) Load() error
func (s *SyncState) Save() error
```

## Default Settings

**IMPORTANT: Auto-sync defaults to OFF**

Users must explicitly enable auto-sync with `caam sync enable`. This prevents unexpected network activity and ensures users understand the feature before using it.

```go
func NewSyncPool() *SyncPool {
    return &SyncPool{
        Enabled:  false,  // Must be explicitly enabled
        AutoSync: false,  // Must be explicitly enabled
        Machines: make(map[string]*Machine),
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Machine type with all fields defined
- [ ] Local identity generation and persistence
- [ ] SyncPool with add/remove/list operations
- [ ] SSH config parser extracts hosts
- [ ] SSH config parser filters known non-caam hosts
- [ ] CSV file location is ~/.caam/sync_machines.csv
- [ ] CSV auto-creation works with helpful comments
- [ ] CSV parsing handles address:port and user@host formats
- [ ] State persistence to disk works
- [ ] Auto-sync defaults to OFF
- [ ] Unit tests for all discovery mechanisms
- [ ] Integration test for pool operations

## Files to Create
- `internal/sync/machine.go`
- `internal/sync/pool.go`
- `internal/sync/discovery.go`
- `internal/sync/csv.go`
- `internal/sync/state.go`
- `internal/sync/identity.go`
- `internal/sync/sync_test.go`

## Dependencies

 {"id":"caam-44i","title":"Manual refresh CLI command","description":"# Manual Refresh CLI Command

## Purpose
Add `caam refresh` command for users to manually trigger token refresh.

## Use Cases
1. User wants to refresh before expiry threshold
2. User wants to refresh all profiles proactively
3. User wants to check if refresh is working
4. Scripting/automation scenarios

## Command Design

### Single Profile Refresh
```bash
$ caam refresh claude work
Refreshing claude/work... done
Token now valid for 59 minutes

$ caam refresh codex main
Refreshing codex/main... done
Token now valid for 1 hour 15 minutes
```

### All Profiles Refresh
```bash
$ caam refresh --all
Refreshing all profiles...
  claude/work         done (59 minutes)
  claude/personal     done (1 hour)
  codex/main          done (1 hour 15 minutes)
  gemini/default      skipped (no refresh token)

3 refreshed, 1 skipped
```

### Dry Run
```bash
$ caam refresh --dry-run
Would refresh:
  claude/work       (expires in 8 minutes)
  claude/personal   (expires in 45 minutes)

Would skip:
  codex/main        (expires in 2 hours)
  gemini/default    (no refresh token)
```

### Force Refresh
```bash
$ caam refresh claude work --force
Refreshing claude/work (forcing even if not expiring)... done
```

## Technical Requirements

### Command Structure
```go
var refreshCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "refresh [provider] [profile]",
    Short: "Refresh OAuth tokens for profiles",
    Long:  `Refresh OAuth tokens before they expire.
    
Without arguments, shows profiles that would benefit from refresh.
With provider and profile, refreshes that specific profile.
With --all, refreshes all profiles that support refresh.`,
    RunE: runRefresh,
}

func init() {
    refreshCmd.Flags().Bool("all", false, "Refresh all profiles")
    refreshCmd.Flags().Bool("dry-run", false, "Show what would be refreshed")
    refreshCmd.Flags().Bool("force", false, "Force refresh even if not expiring")
    refreshCmd.Flags().Bool("quiet", false, "Suppress output")
    rootCmd.AddCommand(refreshCmd)
}
```

### Refresh Logic
```go
func runRefresh(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    all, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("all")
    dryRun, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("dry-run")
    force, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
    
    if all {
        return refreshAllProfiles(cmd.Context(), dryRun, force)
    }
    
    if len(args) < 2 {
        return showRefreshStatus()
    }
    
    provider, profile := args[0], args[1]
    return refreshSingleProfile(cmd.Context(), provider, profile, force)
}
```

### Output Formatting
```go
func formatRefreshResult(provider, profile string, err error, newExpiry time.Time) string {
    prefix := fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", provider, profile)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Sprintf("  %-25s failed: %v", prefix, err)
    }
    ttl := time.Until(newExpiry)
    return fmt.Sprintf("  %-25s done (%s)", prefix, formatDuration(ttl))
}
```

## Error Handling
- Profile not found → error message
- No refresh token → "skipped (no refresh token)"
- Refresh fails → show error, non-zero exit code
- Network error → show error, suggest retry

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `caam refresh provider profile` refreshes single profile
- [ ] `caam refresh --all` refreshes all profiles
- [ ] `caam refresh --dry-run` shows what would happen
- [ ] `caam refresh --force` forces refresh
- [ ] Clear success/failure messages
- [ ] Non-zero exit code on failure
- [ ] Help text documents all options

- `cmd/caam/cmd/refresh.go` (new)

-{"id":"caam-4b5","title":"Define export bundle format and manifest schema","description":"# Task: Define export bundle format and manifest schema

## Context
Before implementing export/import, we need a well-defined bundle format that is:
- Portable across platforms
- Versioned for future compatibility
- Self-describing via manifest
- Verifiable via checksums
- **Optionally encrypted** (auth tokens are sensitive!)

## Bundle Filename Format

### Default (short):
`caam_export_YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM.zip`
Example: `caam_export_2025-12-19_1429.zip`

### Verbose (--verbose-filename flag):
`Exported_Coding_Agent_Account_Auth_Info__As_of__MM_DD_YYYY__HH_MM_AM.zip`
Example: `Exported_Coding_Agent_Account_Auth_Info__As_of__12_19_2025__02_29_PM.zip`

### Encrypted bundles:
Add `.enc` before `.zip`: `caam_export_2025-12-19_1429.enc.zip`

**Rationale**: Short filename is more practical for most users; verbose is available for those who want self-documenting filenames.

## Encryption (Optional)

### When to encrypt
- User passes `--encrypt` flag
- User has `export.encrypt_by_default: true` in config

### Encryption method
- AES-256-GCM (authenticated encryption)
- Key derived from password using Argon2id
- Salt stored in encrypted bundle header

### Bundle structure when encrypted
```
encrypted_bundle.enc.zip
├── .caam_encrypted       # Marker file with encryption metadata
│   {
│     "version": 1,
│     "algorithm": "aes-256-gcm",
│     "kdf": "argon2id",
│     "salt": "base64...",
│     "nonce": "base64..."
│   }
└── payload.enc           # Encrypted zip of actual contents
```

### Decryption on import
- Detect `.caam_encrypted` marker
- Prompt for password (or use --password flag)
- Decrypt payload, extract, continue as normal

## Manifest Schema (v1)

### File: manifest.json
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "caam_version": "1.2.3",
  "export_timestamp": "2025-12-19T14:29:00Z",
  "export_timestamp_human": "December 19, 2025 at 2:29 PM",
  "source": {
    "hostname": "jeffs-macbook",
    "platform": "darwin",
    "arch": "arm64",
    "username": "jeff",
    "caam_data_path": "/Users/jeff/.local/share/caam"
  },
  "contents": {
    "vault": {
      "included": true,
      "profiles": {
        "claude": ["alice@gmail.com", "bob@gmail.com"],
        "codex": ["work@company.com"],
        "gemini": ["personal@gmail.com"]
      },
      "total_profiles": 4
    },
    "config": {
      "included": true,
      "path": "config.yaml"
    },
    "projects": {
      "included": true,
      "path": "projects.json",
      "association_count": 3
    },
    "health": {
      "included": true,
      "path": "health.json"
    },
    "database": {
      "included": false,
      "reason": "user opted out"
    },
    "sync_config": {
      "included": true,
      "path": "sync/",
      "note": "Sync pool and machine configuration"
    }
  },
  "checksums": {
    "algorithm": "sha256",
    "files": {
      "vault/claude/alice@gmail.com/.claude.json": "abc123...",
      "vault/claude/alice@gmail.com/auth.json": "def456...",
      "config.yaml": "ghi789..."
    }
  }
}
```

## Bundle Directory Structure
```
bundle_root/
├── manifest.json
├── vault/
│   ├── claude/
│   │   ├── alice@gmail.com/
│   │   │   ├── .claude.json
│   │   │   ├── auth.json
│   │   │   └── meta.json
│   │   └── bob@gmail.com/
│   │       └── ...
│   ├── codex/
│   │   └── work@company.com/
│   │       └── auth.json
│   └── gemini/
│       └── personal@gmail.com/
│           ├── settings.json
│           └── oauth_credentials.json
├── config.yaml            # Optional
├── projects.json          # Optional
├── health.json            # Optional
├── sync/                  # Optional - sync configuration
│   ├── pool.json
│   └── machines.csv
└── caam.db                # Optional, large
```

## Cross-Platform Path Handling
- All paths in bundle use forward slashes (/)
- On Windows import, convert to backslashes
- Store original platform in manifest for reference
- Relative paths only (no absolute paths in bundle)

## Implementation

### Location
`internal/bundle/` (new package)

### Types
```go
package bundle

// ManifestV1 is the bundle manifest schema version 1
type ManifestV1 struct {
    SchemaVersion        int          `json:"schema_version"`
    CAAMVersion          string       `json:"caam_version"`
    ExportTimestamp      time.Time    `json:"export_timestamp"`
    ExportTimestampHuman string       `json:"export_timestamp_human"`
    Source               SourceInfo   `json:"source"`
    Contents             ContentsInfo `json:"contents"`
    Checksums            ChecksumInfo `json:"checksums"`
}

type SourceInfo struct {
    Hostname     string `json:"hostname"`
    Platform     string `json:"platform"`
    Arch         string `json:"arch"`
    Username     string `json:"username"`
    CAAMDataPath string `json:"caam_data_path"`
}

type ContentsInfo struct {
    Vault      VaultContents    `json:"vault"`
    Config     OptionalContent  `json:"config"`
    Projects   OptionalContent  `json:"projects"`
    Health     OptionalContent  `json:"health"`
    Database   OptionalContent  `json:"database"`
    SyncConfig OptionalContent  `json:"sync_config"`
}

type EncryptionMetadata struct {
    Version   int    `json:"version"`
    Algorithm string `json:"algorithm"`
    KDF       string `json:"kdf"`
    Salt      string `json:"salt"`
    Nonce     string `json:"nonce"`
}
```

### Encryption Functions
```go
func EncryptBundle(plainBundle []byte, password string) ([]byte, *EncryptionMetadata, error)
func DecryptBundle(encBundle []byte, meta *EncryptionMetadata, password string) ([]byte, error)
func IsEncrypted(bundlePath string) (bool, error)
```

### Validation Functions
```go
func ValidateManifest(m *ManifestV1) error
func VerifyChecksums(bundleDir string, manifest *ManifestV1) error
func IsCompatibleVersion(m *ManifestV1) error
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest schema defined as Go types
- [ ] JSON marshaling/unmarshaling works correctly
- [ ] Validation function catches malformed manifests
- [ ] Checksum verification function works
- [ ] Version compatibility check function
- [ ] Encryption/decryption functions work
- [ ] Cross-platform path normalization
- [ ] Unit tests for all functions

## Security Notes
- Warn user that unencrypted bundles contain sensitive tokens
- Suggest encryption for bundles stored in cloud storage
- Never log or display tokens during export/import

- `internal/bundle/validate.go` - Validation functions

 {"id":"caam-4eg","title":"Browser-Profile Aware Login","description":"## Overview
The PRIMARY pain point for users with multiple 'all you can eat' subscriptions is the OAuth login flow. When you have 5 Claude Max accounts (each with a different Google account), the browser OAuth flow is painful because:
1. Browser opens with your 'default' Google account
2. You have to click 'switch account'
3. Find the right account from the list
4. Then authorize

This takes 30-60 seconds and is error-prone (logging into wrong account).

## Solution
Associate each caam profile with a specific browser profile (e.g., Chrome profile). When login flows need a browser, open the URL in THAT browser profile, which already has the correct Google account logged in.

## Key Insight
Chrome (and Firefox) support launching with a specific profile directory. This is the same mechanism that allows people to have separate 'work' and 'personal' browser profiles. We leverage this to make OAuth flows automatically use the right Google account.

## Implementation Approach
1. Add browser profile config to Profile struct (browser_command, browser_profile_dir)
2. Create browser launcher abstraction
3. Detect localhost redirect URLs from CLI stdout
4. Launch browser with correct profile when URL detected
5. Support Chrome, Firefox, and 'default browser' fallback

## User Value
Reduces login friction from 30-60 seconds to ~5 seconds (just click authorize). Eliminates risk of logging into wrong account.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:49:43.32942877-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:22:09.970282351-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:22:09.970282351-05:00","close_reason":"Browser-Profile Aware Login epic complete. All 5 child tasks done: Profile struct browser config, browser launcher abstraction, URL detection, provider integration, and CLI flags."}
 {"id":"caam-4ve","title":"TUI hot reload integration","description":"## Purpose
Integrate the file system watcher with the TUI to automatically refresh the profile
list when files change on disk.

## Background
This enables a seamless workflow where:
1. User has TUI open in Terminal 1
2. User runs `caam backup claude newprofile` in Terminal 2
3. TUI automatically shows the new profile without restart

codex-pool achieves similar with `POST /admin/reload`. For caam, we use file watching.

## User Experience
```
# Terminal 1: TUI running
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────────────────┐
│ ● work@company.com    🟢 Active       │
│   personal@gmail.com  🟡              │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘

# Terminal 2: Add new profile
$ caam backup claude newproject@corp.com
Profile saved.

# Terminal 1: TUI auto-updates (no restart needed!)
┌─ Claude Profiles ─────────────────────┐
│ ● work@company.com       🟢 Active    │
│   personal@gmail.com     🟡           │
│   newproject@corp.com    🟢 NEW       │  ← appeared automatically
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Technical Implementation

### Message Type
```go
// internal/tui/messages.go
type profilesChangedMsg struct {
    event watcher.Event
}
```

### Model Integration
```go
// internal/tui/model.go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields
    watcher *watcher.Watcher
}

// Init starts the file watcher
func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
    return tea.Batch(
        m.loadProfiles(),
        m.watchProfiles(),
    )
}

// watchProfiles returns a command that listens for file changes
func (m Model) watchProfiles() tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        select {
        case event := <-m.watcher.Events():
            return profilesChangedMsg{event: event}
        case err := <-m.watcher.Errors():
            return errMsg{err: err}
        }
    }
}

// Update handles the change event
func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
    switch msg := msg.(type) {
    case profilesChangedMsg:
        // Show brief notification
        m.statusMsg = fmt.Sprintf("Profile %s %s", 
            msg.event.Profile, 
            eventTypeVerb(msg.event.Type))
        
        // Reload profiles and continue watching
        return m, tea.Batch(
            m.loadProfiles(),
            m.watchProfiles(),
        )
    }
}
```

### Visual Feedback
```go
// Show "NEW" badge briefly for added profiles
type profileBadge struct {
    profile  string
    badge    string
    expiry   time.Time
}

func (m *Model) addBadge(profile, badge string, duration time.Duration) {
    m.badges[profile] = profileBadge{
        profile: profile,
        badge:   badge,
        expiry:  time.Now().Add(duration),
    }
}
```

### Graceful Degradation
```go
// If watcher fails, TUI still works but without auto-refresh
func (m Model) Init() tea.Cmd {
    cmds := []tea.Cmd{m.loadProfiles()}
    
    w, err := watcher.New(m.profilesDir)
    if err != nil {
        // Log warning but continue without watching
        log.Printf("Warning: file watching unavailable: %v", err)
    } else {
        m.watcher = w
        cmds = append(cmds, m.watchProfiles())
    }
    
    return tea.Batch(cmds...)
}
```

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/model.go
- internal/tui/messages.go
- internal/tui/profiles_panel.go (for badges)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New profiles appear automatically in TUI
- [ ] Modified profiles trigger refresh
- [ ] Deleted profiles are removed from list
- [ ] Brief notification shows what changed
